pages.full_text:
1      We went back down the valley (no swimming because of a damn) & had lunch down a narrow track (nerve-wracking because of the drop alongside into the river. We all swam in the bluey water. It was freezing. We had lunch.

Bodegas de Sobrarbe (supper in Ainsa) .-
(The Barrel (stone) vaulted roof was typical of church interiors, too.)
   It was a long drive on over the border into France, through a tunnel going upwards, Tunnel of Bielsa. Then on down into France in first gear
pages.notes: The 'l' or '1' in the top-left corner may be a page number continuation marker or partial page number; treated as null since only partial/ambiguous. The text continues from a previous page. 'damn' may read 'dam' (a water dam). Caption text '.-' appears after the caption as punctuation. 'Ainsa' is a town in the Spanish Pyrenees (Sobrarbe region). Tunnel of Bielsa is a road tunnel through the Pyrenees between Spain and France.

pages.full_text:
by   We reached a French Village, __Massat__ * * *. a municipal campsite. Clean warm showers but no pool. We got the last pitch & were so close to the next campers that we smelled their cigarettes all the time. Walking around the village after supper was nice. Good views of a mountain valley.
   __Monday__
   We left for the enormous cave of Bédeilhac . It is so big that an aeroplane flew out of it. During the war the Germans built an aeroplane factory in it. The 1½ hour guided tour was rather energetic, looking at red dots & [?bison] bison, paintings in red, yellow & black & carvings & gougings. All in French.
   We had no bread for lunch so we drove on to a Park of Prehistory, which we very
pages.notes: Page number 29 is clearly visible at top right. 'Massat' is underlined in the original. 'Massat' is a village in Ariège, France (Pyrenees). 'Bédeilhac' is a cave (Grotte de Bédeilhac) near Tarascon-sur-Ariège, Ariège, France. The '* * *' after Massat appears to be some kind of annotation/asterisks by the diarist. The struck-through word before 'bison' is difficult to read — possibly 'bison' written then struck through and rewritten; transcribed as '[?bison] bison'. Text ends mid-sentence — continues on next page. The volume covers 2003–2019; no year is explicitly stated on this spread; based on content (Spain/France Pyrenees trip) year cannot be determined from this page alone.
